settings.title: Date Helpers Settings
settings.description: Configure how dates are inserted and formatted.

locale.name: Locale
locale.desc: Language and region for date formatting (e.g., en, fr, de, es). Use 'auto' to inherit from Obsidian.

weekStart.name: Week starts on
weekStart.desc: First day of the week for calendar displays

enableDatePicker.name: Enable date picker
enableDatePicker.desc: Show calendar picker UI for date selection

enableNLP.name: Enable natural language parsing
enableNLP.desc: Parse date expressions like "tomorrow", "next Monday", "3 days ago"

nlpAutoDetect.name: Auto-detect language
nlpAutoDetect.desc: Automatically detect and parse date expressions. Supported: English, French, German, Japanese, Portuguese, Dutch, Chinese.

nlpStrictMode.name: Parsing mode
nlpStrictMode.desc: Controls how aggressively the parser matches text

nlpShowWarning.name: Show parsing warning
nlpShowWarning.desc: Display a notification when a date expression cannot be parsed. The original text is always preserved.

dailyNotes.description: Configure how Daily Note wikilinks are generated. These settings apply when using the "Insert Daily Note link" or "Open Daily Note" actions.

aliasFormat.name: Wikilink alias format
aliasFormat.desc: Format preset for the display text (alias) in Daily Note wikilinks

createIfMissing.name: Create Daily Note if missing
createIfMissing.desc: Automatically create the Daily Note file when it doesn't exist (uses your Daily Notes template)

text.description: Select default formats for plain text date insertion. These settings apply when using the "Insert date as text" action or preset commands.

defaultDateFormat.name: Default date format
defaultDateFormat.desc: Format used by date preset commands

defaultTimeFormat.name: Default time format
defaultTimeFormat.desc: Format used by time preset commands

defaultDateTimeFormat.name: Default datetime format
defaultDateTimeFormat.desc: Format used by datetime preset commands

triggers.description: Type these character sequences to open the date picker inline. You can configure multiple triggers here. You can also use Obsidian's Hotkeys settings for keyboard shortcuts.

characters.name: Trigger characters
characters.desc: Character sequences that open the date picker (e.g., @@, //d)

validation.tooLong: Trigger must be 5 characters or less
validation.minRequired: At least one trigger is required

presets.description: List of all format presets available for use in commands and settings.

picker.nlpPlaceholder: Type a date expression (e.g., tomorrow, next Monday)
